```{r}
      "Significant improvement" = "#2A9D8F",
      "Moderate improvement"    = "#F0C040",
      "No change"               = "#E07B54",
      "Management worse"        = "#C0392B"
    ),
    guide = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE, nrow = 2)
  ) +
  labs(y = NULL, fill = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    axis.line.x.bottom = element_line(colour = "grey30", linewidth = 0.5),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = rel(1.14), colour = "grey20"),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = rel(1.14), colour = "grey20"),
    axis.title.x       = element_text(size = rel(1), colour = "grey40"),
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    legend.text        = element_text(size = rel(1)),
    plot.margin        = margin(10, 25, 10, 10)
  )
```

As can be seen, while responses vary among FGD groups, most groups agreed that the management of forest resources had improved moderately or significantly under CFM, and only a small number of primarily youth groups considered that it had gotten worse. A very high proportion of community female FDG groups considered that the management of forest resources was significantly better under CFM, a difference that was statistically significant.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
### Forest resources management - FGDs - Model methods

To examine the differences among FGD types in their assessment of whether CFM had improved forest resources management, we employed a cumulative link mixed model (CLMM) with random intercepts for province and CF nested within province. We used FGD type as the factor. We examined the significance of pairwise differences among FGD types using Tukey HSD.
:::

```{r}
#| label: model_frm_fgd
#| include: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| code-fold: true

test.data <- cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type != "individual_interview") |>
  mutate(
    frm = case_match(
      impact_on_sfm_forest_resources_management,
      "no_management_has_worsened"    ~ "Management worse",
      "no_change"                     ~ "No change",
      "yes_moderate_improvement"      ~ "Moderate improvement",
      "yes_significant_improvement"   ~ "Significant improvement",
      .default = NA
    ),
    frm = factor(frm,
                 levels = c("Management worse", "No change",
                            "Moderate improvement", "Significant improvement"),
                 ordered = TRUE),
    interview_type = factor(
      case_match(
        interview_type,
        "cfmg_executive" ~ "Executive",
        "cfmg_female"    ~ "Female",
        "cfmg_male"      ~ "Male",
        "cfmg_youth"     ~ "Youth"
      ),
      levels = c("Executive", "Female", "Male", "Youth")
    )
  ) |>
  drop_na(frm) |>
  mutate(province_cfmg = interaction(province, cfmg_name)) |>
  droplevels()

if (nlevels(test.data$frm) >= 2) {
  M13 <- clmm(frm ~
                interview_type +
                (1 | province) +
                (1 | province_cfmg),
              data = test.data)
  emm.M13      <- emmeans(M13, specs = ~ interview_type)
  pairwise.M13 <- pairs(emm.M13, adjust = "Tukey")
} else {
  M13 <- NULL
  cat("Model not fitted: only", nlevels(test.data$frm),
      "level(s) present in frm after filtering.\n")
}
```

```{r}
#| label: M13_frm_fgd
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| output-fold: true
#| output-summary: "Model results (click to expand)"

if (!is.null(M13)) summary(M13) else cat("Model not fitted.")
```

```{r}
#| label: pairwise_frm_fgd
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| output-fold: true
#| output-summary: "Pairwise comparisons (click to expand)"

if (!is.null(M13)) summary(pairwise.M13) else cat("Model not fitted.")
```

We also asked which specific forest resources had improved under CFM (@fig-harvested_resources).

```{r}
#| label: fig-harvested_resources
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| fig-cap: "Forest resources that have improved under CFM, as reported by female, male, and youth FGDs. Word size is proportional to the number of FGDs reporting an improvement for each resource."
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 7

cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type %in% c("cfmg_female", "cfmg_male", "cfmg_youth")) |>
  select(
    "Pupwe"            = impact_on_sfm_harvested_forest_resources_pupwe,
    "Devil's claw"     = impact_on_sfm_harvested_forest_resources_devils_claw,
    "Fish"             = impact_on_sfm_harvested_forest_resources_fish,
    "Lusala"           = impact_on_sfm_harvested_forest_resources_lusala,
    "Mungongo"         = impact_on_sfm_harvested_forest_resourcesmungongo,
    "Bamboo"           = impact_on_sfm_harvested_forest_resources_bamboo,
    "Munkoyo"          = impact_on_sfm_harvested_forest_resources_munkoyo,
    "Poles"            = impact_on_sfm_harvested_forest_resources_poles,
    "Bushmeat"         = impact_on_sfm_harvested_forest_resources_bushmeat,
    "Timber"           = impact_on_sfm_harvested_forest_resources_timber,
    "Fibres"           = impact_on_sfm_harvested_forest_resources_fibers,
    "Chikanda"         = impact_on_sfm_harvested_forest_resources_chikanda,
    "Firewood"         = interview_grp_impact_on_sfm_harvested_forest_resources_firewood,
    "Thatching grass"  = impact_on_sfm_harvested_forest_resources_thatching_grass,
    "Medicinal plants" = impact_on_sfm_harvested_forest_resources_medicinal_plants,
    "Caterpillars"     = impact_on_sfm_harvested_forest_resources_caterpillars,
    "Honey"            = impact_on_sfm_harvested_forest_resources_honey,
    "Wild fruits"      = impact_on_sfm_harvested_forest_resources_wild_fruits,
    "Mushrooms"        = impact_on_sfm_harvested_forest_resources_mushrooms
  ) |>
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "word", values_to = "value") |>
  filter(!is.na(value)) |>
  group_by(word) |>
  summarise(freq = sum(as.integer(value) == 1, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  filter(freq > 0) |>
  ggplot(aes(label = word, size = freq, colour = freq)) +
  geom_text_wordcloud(eccentricity = 0.65, seed = 42) +
  scale_size(range = c(3, 30)) +
  scale_colour_gradient(low = "#6ABFB8", high = "#1A6B63") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0))
```

Among the individual interviewees (@tbl-frm_individual) the vast majority of interviewees considered that the management of forest resources had improved under CFM. There was a significant difference in responses between community members and DFOs. The DFOs were more likely to suggest there had been no change or only a moderate improvement in forest resource management under CFM, compared to the much higher proportion of interviewees indicating a significant improvement among community representatives. 

```{r}
#| label: tbl-frm_individual
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| tbl-cap: "Perceived change in forest resources management inside the community forest by individual interviewee role."

cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type == "individual_interview") |>
  mutate(
    frm = case_match(
      impact_on_sfm_forest_resources_management,
      "no_management_has_worsened"    ~ "Management worse",
      "no_change"                     ~ "No change",
      "yes_moderate_improvement"      ~ "Moderate improvement",
      "yes_significant_improvement"   ~ "Significant improvement",
      .default = NA
    ),
    frm = factor(frm,
                 levels = c("Management worse", "No change",
                            "Moderate improvement", "Significant improvement")),
    interviewee_role = factor(
      case_match(
        interviewee_role,
        "cfmg_exec_member" ~ "CFMG executive member",
        "cfmg_member"      ~ "Community member",
        "trad_leader"      ~ "Traditional leader",
        "dfo"              ~ "District Forestry Officer",
        "NGO_rep"          ~ "NGO representative"
      ),
      levels = c("CFMG executive member", "Community member", "Traditional leader",
                 "District Forestry Officer", "NGO representative")
    )
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(frm)) |>
  count(interviewee_role, frm, name = "obs") |>
  group_by(interviewee_role) |>
  mutate(n = sum(obs), prop = obs / n) |>
  ungroup() |>
  select(interviewee_role, n, frm, prop) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = frm, values_from = prop, values_fill = 0) |>
  arrange(interviewee_role) |>
  select(interviewee_role, n,
         `Management worse`, `No change`, `Moderate improvement`, `Significant improvement`) |>
  gt() |>
  cols_label(interviewee_role = "Role") |>
  fmt_percent(columns = c(`Management worse`, `No change`, `Moderate improvement`, `Significant improvement`), decimals = 0) |>
  tab_header(
    title    = "Perceived improvement in forest resources management by interviewee role",
    subtitle = "Values represent the % of respondents in each response category. n = the total number of interview responses."
  ) |>
  opt_align_table_header(align = "left") |>
  cols_align(align = "center", columns = -interviewee_role) |>
  cols_align(align = "left", columns = interviewee_role) |>
  tab_style(
    style     = cell_text(align = "center"),
    locations = cells_column_labels(columns = interviewee_role)
  ) |>
  sub_missing()
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
### Forest resources management - Individual interviewees - Model methods

To examine differences among individual interviewees in their assessment of whether CFM had improved forest resources management, we employed a cumulative link mixed model (CLMM) with random intercepts for province and CF nested within province. We included interviewee role, interviewee gender and interviewee age as factors. To examine pairwise differences among interviewee roles, we employed Tukey HSD.
:::

```{r}
#| label: test_data_frm_individual
#| echo: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| code-fold: true

test.data <- cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type == "individual_interview") |>
  mutate(
    frm = case_match(
      impact_on_sfm_forest_resources_management,
      "no_management_has_worsened"    ~ "Management worse",
      "no_change"                     ~ "No change",
      "yes_moderate_improvement"      ~ "Moderate improvement",
      "yes_significant_improvement"   ~ "Significant improvement",
      .default = NA
    ),
    frm = factor(frm,
                 levels = c("Management worse", "No change",
                            "Moderate improvement", "Significant improvement"),
                 ordered = TRUE),
    interviewee_gender = demo_information_interviewee_gender,
    interviewee_age    = demo_information_interviewee_age
  ) |>
  drop_na(frm) |>
  mutate(province_cfmg = interaction(province, cfmg_name)) |>
  droplevels()

if (nlevels(test.data$frm) >= 2) {
  M14 <- clmm(frm ~
                 interviewee_role +
                 interviewee_gender +
                 interviewee_age +
                 (1 | province) +
                 (1 | province_cfmg),
               data = test.data)
  emm.M14      <- emmeans(M14, specs = ~ interviewee_role)
  pairwise.M14 <- pairs(emm.M14, adjust = "Tukey")
} else {
  M14 <- NULL
  cat("Model not fitted: only", nlevels(test.data$frm),
      "level(s) present in frm after filtering.\n")
}
```

```{r}
#| label: M14_frm_individual
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| output-fold: true
#| output-summary: "Model results (click to expand)"

if (!is.null(M14)) summary(M14) else cat("Model not fitted.")
```

```{r}
#| label: pairwise_frm_individual
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| output-fold: true
#| output-summary: "Pairwise comparisons (click to expand)"

if (!is.null(M14)) summary(pairwise.M14) else cat("Model not fitted.")
```

### Restoration

We asked CFMG executives whether natural forest restoration is practiced inside the community forest. We restrict the responses for this question to CFMG Executives, because this is the groups that should be best informed about the activities of the CFMG (and the differences in responses among groups to this question are no of substantive interest).

```{r}
#| label: fig-natural_forest_restoration
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| fig-cap: "Whether CFMGs undertake natural forest restoration in the community forest by province. The length of the bars represents the number of CFMGs either undertaking restoration (Yes) or not (No). The numbers on the bars represent the actual number of CFMGs in each category."

cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type == "cfmg_executive") |>
  mutate(
    response = substr(impact_on_sfm_natural_forest_restoration, 1, 1),
    response = case_match(
      response,
      "y" ~ "Yes",
      "n" ~ "No"
    )
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(response)) |>
  count(province, response) |>
  mutate(
    n_signed = if_else(response == "No", -n, n),
    response  = factor(response, levels = c("No", "Yes"))
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(y = reorder(province, n_signed), x = n_signed, fill = response)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.65, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, colour = "white", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = n,
        hjust = if_else(response == "No", 1.4, -0.4)),
    size = 3.5, fontface = "bold", colour = "grey25"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = ~abs(.x),
    expand = expansion(mult = 0.18),
    name   = "Number of CFMGs"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("No" = "#E07B54", "Yes" = "#2A9D8F"),
    guide  = guide_legend(reverse = FALSE)
  ) +
  labs(
    y    = NULL,
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    axis.line.x.bottom = element_line(colour = "grey30", linewidth = 0.5),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = rel(1.14), colour = "grey20"),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = rel(1.14), colour = "grey20"),
    axis.title.x       = element_text(size = rel(1), colour = "grey40"),
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    legend.text        = element_text(size = rel(1)),
    plot.margin        = margin(10, 25, 10, 10)
  )
```

Natural forest restoration is being practiced across approximately half of the CFs in our sample, mostly in Muchinga, Central and Northern provinces. We also asked CFMG executives about the specific restoration methods applied inside their CF. The majority were conserving natural regeneration. Only one CFMG was involved in planting native species and removal of invasives was not practiced.

```{r}
#| label: fig-restoration_methods
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| fig-cap: "Restoration methods applied inside the community forest, as reported by CFMG executives. The length of the bars represents the number of CFMGs either using (Yes) or not using (No) each method. The numbers on the bars represent the actual number of CFMGs in each category."
#| fig-height: 4

cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type == "cfmg_executive") |>
  select(
    "Protecting natural regeneration" = impact_on_sfm_restoration_methods_applied_protection,
    "Planting indigenous"             = impact_on_sfm_restoration_methods_applied_planting_with_indigineous_species,
    "Invasives removal"               = impact_on_sfm_restoration_methods_applied_removal
  ) |>
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "method", values_to = "value") |>
  filter(!is.na(value)) |>
  mutate(
    response = case_match(as.character(value), "1" ~ "Yes", "0" ~ "No"),
    response = factor(response, levels = c("No", "Yes")),
    method   = factor(method, levels = c("Invasives removal",
                                         "Planting indigenous",
                                         "Protecting natural regeneration"))
  ) |>
  count(method, response) |>
  mutate(n_signed = if_else(response == "No", -n, n)) |>
  ggplot(aes(y = method, x = n_signed, fill = response)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.65, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, colour = "white", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = n,
        hjust = if_else(response == "No", 1.4, -0.4)),
    size = 3.5, fontface = "bold", colour = "grey25"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = ~abs(.x),
    expand = expansion(mult = 0.18),
    name   = "Number of CFMGs"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("No" = "#E07B54", "Yes" = "#2A9D8F"),
    guide  = guide_legend(reverse = FALSE)
  ) +
  labs(y = NULL, fill = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    axis.line.x.bottom = element_line(colour = "grey30", linewidth = 0.5),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = rel(1.14), colour = "grey20"),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = rel(1.14), colour = "grey20"),
    axis.title.x       = element_text(size = rel(1), colour = "grey40"),
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    legend.text        = element_text(size = rel(1)),
    plot.margin        = margin(10, 25, 10, 10)
  )
```

### Plantations

We also asked CFMG executives whether any plantations has been established inside the community forest.

```{r}
#| label: fig-plantation_establishment
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| fig-cap: "Whether CFMGs have established a plantation inside the community forest, by province. The length of the bars represents the number of CFMGs either with (Yes) or without (No) a plantation. The numbers on the bars represent the actual number of CFMGs in each category."

cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type == "cfmg_executive") |>
  mutate(
    response = substr(impact_on_sfm_planted_forest_establishment, 1, 1),
    response = case_match(
      response,
      "y" ~ "Yes",
      "n" ~ "No"
    )
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(response)) |>
  count(province, response) |>
  mutate(
    n_signed = if_else(response == "No", -n, n),
    response  = factor(response, levels = c("No", "Yes"))
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(y = reorder(province, n_signed), x = n_signed, fill = response)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.65, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, colour = "white", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = n,
        hjust = if_else(response == "No", 1.4, -0.4)),
    size = 3.5, fontface = "bold", colour = "grey25"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = ~abs(.x),
    expand = expansion(mult = 0.18),
    name   = "Number of CFMGs"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("No" = "#E07B54", "Yes" = "#2A9D8F"),
    guide  = guide_legend(reverse = FALSE)
  ) +
  labs(y = NULL, fill = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    axis.line.x.bottom = element_line(colour = "grey30", linewidth = 0.5),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = rel(1.14), colour = "grey20"),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = rel(1.14), colour = "grey20"),
    axis.title.x       = element_text(size = rel(1), colour = "grey40"),
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    legend.text        = element_text(size = rel(1)),
    plot.margin        = margin(10, 25, 10, 10)
  )
```

We also asked executives to estimate the number of hectares planted in the plantation. A small number (~9% of the sample) had established tree plantation inside their CF. However, the hectares planted were small (@fig-hectares_planted).

```{r}
#| label: fig-hectares_planted
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| fig-cap: "Estimated area of plantation established inside the community forest, as reported by CFMG executives (n = 6 CFMGs; total area = 20.5 ha). The horizontal bar is the median and the red dot is the mean. Species planted include *Gliricidia sepium* (n = 3 CFMGs), *Pinus* spp. (n = 2), *Acacia* spp. (n = 1), *Faidherbia albidia* (n = 1), and miombo species (n = 1)."
#| fig-width: 4
#| fig-height: 5

cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type == "cfmg_executive") |>
  filter(!is.na(impact_on_sfm_hectares_planted)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = "", y = impact_on_sfm_hectares_planted)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.4, outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.1, alpha = 0.6) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 3, col = "red") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x  = element_text(size = rel(1.14)),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = rel(1.14)),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = rel(1.29))
  ) +
  xlab("") +
  ylab("Hectares planted")
```

### Agroforestry

Last, we asked CFMG executives whether CFM has involved introducing agroforestry practices. In this context, agroforestry is implemented on agricultural land as a means for achieving sustainable intensification and reducing pressure on forests. Around 40% of CFMGs said that agroforestry had been introduced under CFM.

```{r}
#| label: fig-yn_agroforestry
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| fig-cap: "Whether agroforestry is employed to enhance SFM, as reported by CFMG executives. The length of the bars represents the number of CFMGs reporting either a positive impact (Yes) or no impact (No). The numbers on the bars represent the actual number of CFMGs in each category."

cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type == "cfmg_executive") |>
  mutate(
    response = substr(impact_on_sfm_yn_agroforestry, 1, 1),
    response = case_match(
      response,
      "y" ~ "Yes",
      "n" ~ "No"
    )
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(response)) |>
  count(province, response) |>
  mutate(
    n_signed = if_else(response == "No", -n, n),
    response  = factor(response, levels = c("No", "Yes"))
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(y = reorder(province, n_signed), x = n_signed, fill = response)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.65, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, colour = "white", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = n,
        hjust = if_else(response == "No", 1.4, -0.4)),
    size = 3.5, fontface = "bold", colour = "grey25"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = ~abs(.x),
    expand = expansion(mult = 0.18),
    name   = "Number of CFMGs"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("No" = "#E07B54", "Yes" = "#2A9D8F"),
    guide  = guide_legend(reverse = FALSE)
  ) +
  labs(y = NULL, fill = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    axis.line.x.bottom = element_line(colour = "grey30", linewidth = 0.5),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = rel(1.14), colour = "grey20"),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = rel(1.14), colour = "grey20"),
    axis.title.x       = element_text(size = rel(1), colour = "grey40"),
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    legend.text        = element_text(size = rel(1)),
    plot.margin        = margin(10, 25, 10, 10)
  )
```

We also asked CFMG executives about the types of agroforestry practices present in the community.

```{r}
#| label: fig-agroforestry_types
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| fig-cap: "Types of agroforestry practices reported by CFMG executives (n = 30 CFMGs). The length of the bars represents the number of CFMGs either reporting (Yes) or not reporting (No) each practice."
#| fig-height: 4.5

cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type == "cfmg_executive") |>
  select(
    "Silvopasture"               = impact_on_sfm_types_agroforestry_practices_Silvopasture,
    "Windbreaks"                 = impact_on_sfm_types_agroforestry_practices_wind_breaks,
    "Home gardens"               = impact_on_sfm_types_agroforestry_practices_home_gardens,
    "Boundary planting/woodlots" = impact_on_sfm_types_agroforestry_practices_boundary_planting_woodlots,
    "Intercropping"              = impact_on_sfm_types_agroforestry_practices_intercropping
  ) |>
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "practice", values_to = "value") |>
  filter(!is.na(value)) |>
  mutate(
    response = case_match(as.character(value), "1" ~ "Yes", "0" ~ "No"),
    response = factor(response, levels = c("No", "Yes")),
    practice = factor(practice, levels = c(
      "Silvopasture", "Windbreaks", "Home gardens",
      "Boundary planting/woodlots", "Intercropping"
    ))
  ) |>
  count(practice, response) |>
  mutate(n_signed = if_else(response == "No", -n, n)) |>
  ggplot(aes(y = practice, x = n_signed, fill = response)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.65, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, colour = "white", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = n,
        hjust = if_else(response == "No", 1.4, -0.4)),
    size = 3.5, fontface = "bold", colour = "grey25"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = ~abs(.x),
    expand = expansion(mult = 0.18),
    name   = "Number of CFMGs"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("No" = "#E07B54", "Yes" = "#2A9D8F"),
    guide  = guide_legend(reverse = FALSE)
  ) +
  labs(y = NULL, fill = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    axis.line.x.bottom = element_line(colour = "grey30", linewidth = 0.5),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = rel(1.14), colour = "grey20"),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = rel(1.14), colour = "grey20"),
    axis.title.x       = element_text(size = rel(1), colour = "grey40"),
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    legend.text        = element_text(size = rel(1)),
    plot.margin        = margin(10, 25, 10, 10)
  )
```

We also asked CFMG executives about the species planted in agroforestry systems. Among the 30 CFMGs practicing agroforestry most were employing intercropping with leguminous shrubs for soil heal management, but planting boundary trees or woodlots and homegardens were also popular interventions. 

Among the species used (@fig-agroforestry_species), we can see that *Gliricidia* and *Faidherbia*, leguminous shrubs and trees, respectively, were the most popular.

```{r}
#| label: fig-agroforestry_species
#| code-fold: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| error:   false
#| fig-cap: "Agroforestry species planted, as reported by CFMG executives (n = 30 CFMGs). The length of the bars represents the number of CFMGs either planting (Yes) or not planting (No) each species."
#| fig-height: 4

cfmg |>
  filter(interview_type == "cfmg_executive") |>
  select(
    "Bamboo"             = impact_on_sfm_agroforestry_species_planted_bamboo,
    "Moringa"            = impact_on_sfm_agroforestry_species_planted_moringa,
    "Exotic fruit trees" = impact_on_sfm_agroforestry_species_planted_exotic_fruit_tree,
    "Faidherbia albidia" = impact_on_sfm_agroforestry_species_planted_faidherbia_albidia,
    "Gliricidia sepium"  = impact_on_sfm_agroforestry_species_planted_gliricidia_sepium
  ) |>
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "species", values_to = "value") |>
  filter(!is.na(value)) |>
  mutate(
    response = case_match(as.character(value), "1" ~ "Yes", "0" ~ "No"),
    response = factor(response, levels = c("No", "Yes")),
    species  = factor(species, levels = c(
      "Bamboo", "Moringa", "Exotic fruit trees",
      "Faidherbia albidia", "Gliricidia sepium"
    ))
  ) |>
  count(species, response) |>
  mutate(n_signed = if_else(response == "No", -n, n)) |>
  ggplot(aes(y = species, x = n_signed, fill = response)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.65, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, colour = "white", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = n,
        hjust = if_else(response == "No", 1.4, -0.4)),
    size = 3.5, fontface = "bold", colour = "grey25"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = ~abs(.x),
    expand = expansion(mult = 0.18),
    name   = "Number of CFMGs"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("No" = "#E07B54", "Yes" = "#2A9D8F"),
    guide  = guide_legend(reverse = FALSE)
  ) +
  labs(y = NULL, fill = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    axis.line.x.bottom = element_line(colour = "grey30", linewidth = 0.5),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = rel(1.14), colour = "grey20",
                                      face  = "italic"),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = rel(1.14), colour = "grey20"),
    axis.title.x       = element_text(size = rel(1), colour = "grey40"),
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    legend.text        = element_text(size = rel(1)),
    plot.margin        = margin(10, 25, 10, 10)
  )
```

## Discussion

All the indicators of SFM point to a situation that is improving under CFM, including deforestation, charcoal production, fire frequency, wildlife abundance and natural resource management. Interestingly, there does not appear to be any indication of leakage for most CFs. Most FDG groups indicated that both deforestation and charcoal production were also decreasing outside their CF. There were some differences in responses among the different FDG groups, with community female FDGs tending to have a more positive view of the impact of CFM compared to the other groups. This is interesting and may indicate that under CFM females have more of a voice in forest and forest resource management. Traditionally, women are highly dependent on forest resources, but usually have little say in forest management decisions. It would be worth following up this study to investigate what is driving this positive take on CFM among community women.

Among the individual interviewees, NGO representatives and DFOs tended to have a somewhat more conservative view with respect to positive changes, as compared to individuals from the communities. This may reflect that these professionals have a broader perspective from which to understand changes in forest and forest resource management. However, it could also arise from the fact that they are not as intimately involved in the forest management and hence less aware of the changes. Ultimately, monitoring over a longer period will enable better understanding of how CFM is changing forest management.

Overall, CFM appears to be engendering a change towards improved SFM. However, it is important to note that a small number of FDG groups and individual interviewees suggested the situation was worsening in their CFs. For the success of CFM in Zambia, it will be important to identify CFs that are doing less well, diagnose the causes and implement changes.

Some CFMGs were practicing forest restoration, mostly through protecting natural regeneration, and a few were establishing plantations. However, these activities were not widespread among CFMGs. Restoration - especially through protection of natural regeneration - should integral to CFM and is probably occurring widely simply through improvements in forest protection. As this is an important indicator of SFM and there are government targets to restore 2,000,000 ha by 2030, the FD should integrate restoration targets into forest management plans for CFMGs and monitor outcomes. Agroforestry was also not widely practiced by CFMGs, but is recognised tool for restoration of degraded agricultural land, and can reduce pressure on forests. Plantations are also an important opportunity for CFM. Plantations can supply local building and fuelwood needs, thereby reducing pressure on natural forests. Through sustainable charcoal programmes, they can also supply energy for urban dwellers and again reduce pressure on natural forests. Last, Zambia has an acute shortage of high quality timber for telegraph poles and construction and imports large amounts of wood. A focus on developing plantations through CFM would go some way to meeting these needs, as well as generating a valuable additional source of income for poor rural communities.

In conclusion, our data suggest that CFM in Zambia is driving substantial improvements in SFM, reducing deforestation, charcoal production, and fire frequency, increasing wildlife populations, and improving natural resource management. However, it is important to note that these changes were not ubiquitous across all CFs. While some CFMGs are practising restoration and establishing plantations, there is a substantial opportunity to expand the role of CFM in meeting national restoration and plantation targets.
